title: The Weekly Bottom Line
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: The S&P 500 reached an all-time high before retracing some gains; oil prices spiked in March but have since declined; inflation expectations in Canada have rise
excerpt: The S&P 500 reached an all-time high before retracing some gains; oil prices spiked in March but have since declined; inflation expectations in Canada have risen.

content.introduction: The S&P 500 reached an all-time high before retracing some gains; oil prices spiked in March but have since declined; inflation expectations in Canada have risen.
body: # Our summary of recent economic events and what to expect in the weeks ahead. | U.S. Automotive Outlook 2026: Energy Price Shock Raises New Questions for Industry |. | Revisiting Adjustable-Rate Mortgages (ARMs): A Potential Leg Up in Today’s High-Rate U.S. Housing Market |. | Every Time is Different: 2026’s Energy Shock |. | This Week in the Markets |. The S&P 500 started falling after the conflict began in the beginning of March, reaching a trough near the end of March. Oil prices spike up and then continued rising with volatility through March, peaking at ~$113/barrel in early April. Since then, they have been declining, and the S&P 500 reached a peak this week of over 7100, an all-time high, before retracing some of its gains. Early in the week, Iran announced that the Strait of Hormuz would be reopened to commercial shipping vessels during a newly brokered ceasefire, triggering a sharp pullback in oil prices and a relief rally in risk assets.

# Our summary of recent economic events and what to expect in the weeks ahead. ## U.S. Highlights. | Revisiting Adjustable-Rate Mortgages (ARMs): A Potential Leg Up in Today’s High-Rate U.S. Housing Market |. | This Week in the Markets |. Chart 1 is a line chart showing the average of Canada’s core inflation measures (CPI-Median and CPI-Trim), shown as 3-month annualized percent change, from September 2025 to March 2026. It than moderates to 0.7% in February 2026, before rising to 1.3% in March 2026. Oil prices remain volatile, with WTI up this week amid limited progress on diplomatic efforts between Iran and the U.S. Canadian bond yields also edged higher (as of writing), with Middle East tensions keeping inflation risks in focus. Chart 2 shows Canadian inflation expectations at the one-year, two-year and five-year ahead horizons, according to the Bank of Canada's Business Leaders Pulse Survey. One year ahead expectations have risen from 3% in February to 3.8% in the week of March 15 – 31st.
